non_affiliation.required_text: LegalForecastBench is an independent project. Harvey AI, Harvey LAB, and LegalQuants are not sponsors, partners, or endorsers of this work.
non_affiliation.credit_rule: Credit Harvey LAB by repository, pinned revision, and license. Credit LegalQuants or Jamie Tso only for public feedback that they supplied; do not imply review, approval, sponsorship, or partnership.

owners.D0: Cycle 1 report and leaderboard shell, then official result presentation
owners.D1: Harness writeup, README landing page, and methods preprint draft
owners.D2: Audience and claims governance, LegalQuants drafts, issue mapping, and Beads evidence; never sends externally
owners.D3: CI and quality integration plus independent cross-worktree review
owners.John: Final external communication, release, authorship, and submission decisions

approvals.john-external-send: John explicitly approves the exact message before any external send
approvals.john-preprint-submission: John separately approves authorship and SSRN or arXiv submission
approvals.official-publication: LegalForecastBench-5qd6.41 is complete, independent artifact and claims audit passes, protected publication succeeds, and John approves release
approvals.tier0-publication: The frozen Tier-0 specification predates spend, the reviewed machine package passes independent privacy and claims scans, and John approves release
approvals.tier1-community-publication: Contributor-grade acceptance, trusted score verification, package validation, clean rebuild, independent review, and John release approval all pass

practitioners.call_to_action: Read the human report first, compare score, coverage, cost, tokens, and time, then open the methods and audit evidence before using a result to choose a workflow or model.

ai-researchers.call_to_action: Inspect the contamination boundary, frozen treatment identities, compatibility key, evaluator separation, uncertainty, and immutable evidence; propose a preregistered replication or extension.

legalquants.call_to_action: Review the preliminary result and offer pilot-design input before the declared input window closes and before any stratified-pilot score is observed.

contributors.call_to_action: Reproduce the pinned fixture path, follow the contributor-grade submission contract, validate the package locally, and submit an evidence-linked pull request.

preliminary.required_label: Preliminary — one task pair, operator-run, not independently reproducible
required_evidence: A frozen pre-spend task, arm, model, settings, containment, resource, order, retry, evaluator, metric, and claims specification
Hash-identical solver-visible bytes and physical solver/evaluator separation
Sealed deliverables evaluated separately with every attempt and failure retained
Independent arithmetic, privacy, credential, path, transcript, grader-canary, and claims scans
Peer reporting of score, selected/solved/evaluated coverage, cost basis, exposed token dimensions, wall-clock, attempts, retries, and failures
permitted_claims: A plumbing result for the pinned task and run
An observed paired difference only when the complete compatibility key matches
Separately labeled system-bundle observations when the compatibility key does not match

required_evidence: Contributor-grade whole-process containment and solver/evaluator canaries
Explicit fail-closed auth, receipt, resume, cancellation, redaction, and capability identities
Trusted score verification, hostile package validation, and clean-checkout aggregate and site rebuild
Pinned contributor documentation and immutable submission evidence
permitted_claims: The named package is reproducible through the documented contributor path
Observed score, coverage, efficiency, and failure results for the declared task set and treatment identity
Pilot estimates only when the pilot was frozen before scores and reports supported uncertainty
forbidden_claims: harness causality without the complete matched key and prespecified pilot
overall winner across LegalForecast-MTD and Harvey LAB

required_evidence: Completed exact-100 freeze, dispatch, authenticated receipts, fan-in, aggregate, and publication gates
Frozen registry, eligibility anchor, cohort, labels, packets, budgets, and run identity
Independent artifact, arithmetic, claims, privacy, and disclosure audit
Headline micro-Brier results with clustered uncertainty, coverage, accounting, model identity, and limitations
permitted_claims: Relative performance of frozen Cycle 1 model configurations on the shared exact-100 LegalForecast-MTD cohort
Audited Cycle 1 score, coverage, cost, token, time, cadence, and failure facts
Methodological claims supported by the frozen artifacts and declared design
forbidden_claims: capability improvement across cycles with different case mixes
cross-suite ranking against Harvey LAB rubric scores
overall winner across official and community tracks

required_disclosures: Official tier label
Exact-100 cohort and Cycle 1 identity
Score, uncertainty, coverage, accounting, model identity, baseline context, audit link, and limitations
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Read the report, then follow the immutable audit and methods links before interpreting model differences.

required_disclosures: Official tier label
Metric direction and clustered uncertainty
Coverage, baseline context, and link to the full report
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Compare frozen Cycle 1 rows only, then read the report for uncertainty, exclusions, and limits.

required_disclosures: Exact preliminary label
Matched-key result or system-bundle mismatch
Task and arm identities, score, coverage, cost, tokens, wall-clock, attempts, retries, failures, and evaluator limits
Issue 49 remains open and the result is not contributor-safe
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Inspect the pinned specification and machine evidence, then suggest a preregistered pilot improvement before the input window closes.

required_disclosures: Exact preliminary label
Separate Codex addendum and treatment identity
Exact compatibility outcome, score, coverage, cost, tokens, wall-clock, attempts, retries, and failures
No Claude-versus-Codex ranking and no claim that Codex delayed the Claude result
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Compare disclosed system bundles and compatibility facts without treating the follow-on as a general harness ranking.

required_disclosures: Reproducible community tier label
Non-official identity
Task, harness, model, evaluator, compatibility, coverage, efficiency, attempts, failures, and immutable package links
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Open the validated package and reproduction instructions before comparing community rows.

required_disclosures: Official tier label next to the result link
Cycle identity, headline metric, and report link
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Open the audited Cycle 1 report and methods rather than relying on the landing-page summary.

required_disclosures: Exact preliminary label next to the result link
One-task scope and non-official identity
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Read the full writeup and frozen specification before interpreting the observed difference.

required_disclosures: Reproducible community tier label
Non-official identity and contributor documentation link
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Run the pinned fixture, validate a package, and follow the contributor submission path.

required_disclosures: Official tier label for Cycle 1 evidence
Methods, contamination limits, uncertainty, audit references, and limitations
Independent tier labels for any community appendix result
public_surfaces.call_to_action: Review the methods and immutable artifacts; cite the released version and disclose whether any community appendix is preliminary or reproducible.

controlled_communications.required_boundary: Describe the intended native-tools design as pending feasibility, make no unpublished result claim, invite input before pilot selection freezes and before pilot scores exist, and include the non-affiliation text.
Link only a validated publication, reproduce its exact evidence-tier label, state the one-task scope, and invite pilot input before the declared window closes.
Repository drafting does not authorize submission; John separately approves authorship, destination, final text, and release.

calendar.miss_action: Record the blocker and revised forecast on the live Bead before any public-surface work proceeds.
Record whether John approved a send, declined to send, or deferred the decision; do not send without approval and do not let the decision block engineering.
Move later model candidates to Cycle 2 unless John records a new schedule decision before freeze.
Escalate the exact blocker and publish only verified plumbing evidence with the preliminary label if a matched result is unavailable.

calendar.relative_deadline: Within 24 hours after the validated Tier-0 publication; 2026-07-22 is the projection from the package target.
calendar.miss_action: Record why the writeup or README link missed the required 24-hour post-validation window and assign a new owner/date.
Record the blocker and continue the Claude publication and Tier-1 freeze without waiting for Codex.
Update the live official critical Bead with the source shortfall, revised forecast, and impact on exact-100 readiness.
Escalate the failing contributor-grade gate and keep Tier 0 explicitly preliminary.
Record the failed acceptance gate; do not close issue 49 or relabel Tier-0 evidence.
Record the immutable-gate shortfall and revised smoke forecast without changing the frozen cohort rule.
Record the exact smoke failure and required corrective action; do not dispatch the official matrix.
Close the window as feedback received, no response, or John-declined-send; do not leave the pilot indefinitely blocked.
Record the blocking immutable gate and revised schedule; do not alter the cohort, registry, or approval requirements.
Record the audit or publication blocker and keep all result surfaces private or clearly unreleased.

calendar.relative_deadline: Within 24 hours after audited official publication; 2026-08-18 is the projection from the publication target.
calendar.miss_action: Record why the README missed the 24-hour result-link window and assign an immediate corrective owner.
Record the prespecified pilot gate or execution failure; do not generalize from Tier 0 instead.

calendar.relative_deadline: Within seven days after the audited official report; 2026-08-24 is the projection from the publication target.
calendar.miss_action: Record the drafting blocker; retain audited result links and do not substitute unreviewed claims.
John must explicitly continue the valid frozen universe, reanchor and reproject before output, or defer Cycle 1.

calendar.relative_deadline: Within fourteen days after the audited official report; 2026-08-31 is the projection from the publication target.
calendar.miss_action: Record the packaging blocker; repository completion still does not authorize submission without John's separate approval.
